title: Miami’s Best Sushi Restaurants
summary: Miami offers no shortage of excellent places to satisfy a sushi craving, whether you’re looking for traditional sushi counters, omakase destinations, Nikkei restaurants, or inventive newcomers. These are the city’s standout sushi spots for a quick lunch, a splurge-worthy dinner,…
whatHappening: Eater Miami has compiled a guide to the city's best sushi restaurants, showcasing Miami's diverse sushi scene. The roundup highlights everything from traditional sushi counters to high-end omakase destinations and innovative Nikkei fusion spots.
whoInvolved: Eater Miami, a leading food and dining publication covering the South Florida restaurant scene, has curated this list of sushi establishments throughout the city. The featured restaurants range from established fine-dining omakase venues to newer, trend-forward concepts.
whyMatters: This guide helps Miami diners navigate an increasingly competitive sushi market and discover quality options across different price points and dining experiences. It reflects Miami's growing culinary sophistication and the city's appeal as a destination for Japanese cuisine beyond traditional offerings.
moreContext: Miami's sushi scene has evolved significantly over the past decade, driven by the city's large Japanese expatriate community, affluent demographics, and status as a major international hub. Beyond traditional sushi bars, restaurants have embraced Nikkei cuisine—the fusion of Japanese and Peruvian flavors—which has gained popularity in Miami's dining circles. The market now includes everything from casual lunch spots to ultra-premium omakase experiences where diners pay upwards of $200 per person. This diversity reflects broader trends in American fine dining where Japanese cuisine continues to gain prestige and investment.

title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour is emerging as Miami Beach's premier alternative to the chaotic Ocean Drive scene. This bay-side neighborhood is attracting locals and visitors seeking a wellness-focused, walkable lifestyle in a vibrant yet accessible setting.
whoInvolved: Sunset Harbour residents and local businesses are driving the neighborhood's transformation. Miami Curated, a lifestyle publication, is documenting and promoting this shift in how people experience Miami Beach.
whyMatters: This trend signals a broader shift in travel and lifestyle preferences toward authentic, community-oriented experiences over mass tourism. The success of Sunset Harbour could reshape Miami Beach's economic and cultural identity, making it more appealing to affluent residents and quality-conscious visitors seeking wellness and local character.
moreContext: Sunset Harbour occupies Miami Beach's quieter bay side, positioning it as a direct counterpoint to the party-centric, tourist-saturated Ocean Drive corridor. The neighborhood emphasizes walkability, local dining, wellness amenities, and a more refined atmosphere than South Beach's traditional nightlife district. This reflects broader urbanization trends favoring mixed-use, pedestrian-friendly neighborhoods with authentic community identity. The shift also aligns with post-pandemic preferences for outdoor spaces, health-conscious activities, and experiences that prioritize quality over volume.

title: Taste the Gables is Back with 75 Restaurants
summary: The City of Coral Gables’ popular restaurant month, Taste the Gables, returns this July with its most expansive lineup to date. More than 75 restaurants, ranging from Michelin-recognized destinations to neighborhood favorites and varied cuisines, are participating, showcasing…
whatHappening: Taste the Gables, Coral Gables' signature restaurant month, is returning this July with 75 participating restaurants—the largest lineup in the event's history. The participating venues span from Michelin-recognized fine dining to casual neighborhood spots across diverse cuisines.
whoInvolved: The City of Coral Gables is organizing the event, partnering with local restaurants ranging from high-end establishments to casual eateries throughout the community. The event showcases the city's diverse dining scene and culinary reputation.
whyMatters: The expanded restaurant roster drives foot traffic and revenue for local dining establishments during a traditionally slower summer month. For diners, it offers accessible tasting opportunities at prestigious restaurants and a chance to discover neighborhood favorites at promotional prices.
moreContext: Taste the Gables is an annual dining event that has become a marquee offering for Coral Gables' hospitality sector. The expansion to 75 restaurants reflects growing participation from the local culinary community and increased consumer interest in the event. Restaurant weeks and similar promotional events have become standard marketing tools for destination dining cities, helping venues attract new customers and maintain summer revenue. This year's record participation suggests both strong post-pandemic recovery in the local restaurant industry and the event's growing prominence as a cultural initiative.

title: New Restaurant Reviews + More Eating News: Part 1
summary: Reviews of new restaurant openings including Casa Tua Cucina Wynwood, "Meet me at Milos "spritz and aperitivo special, a Dubai chewy cookie, new top spot for coffee lovers with a Norwegian dessert, first Cuban restaurant to get a Michelin star and a bundle for non alcoholic…
whatHappening: Miami's restaurant scene is experiencing a wave of new openings and accolades, from Casa Tua Cucina Wynwood to a historic first Michelin star for a Cuban restaurant. The dining landscape is expanding with diverse concepts ranging from aperitivo bars to specialty coffee shops featuring Norwegian desserts.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated is covering multiple restaurant venues and operators in the Miami area, including Casa Tua Cucina in Wynwood, Milos bar, and an unnamed Cuban restaurant that achieved Michelin recognition. These establishments represent various ownership and culinary teams operating in Miami's competitive food and beverage sector.
whyMatters: These developments signal Miami's growing recognition as a serious culinary destination beyond casual dining, particularly with the first Cuban restaurant earning a Michelin star—a validation of the city's cultural food traditions. The variety of new concepts attracts both locals and tourists, strengthening Miami's position in the national restaurant market and supporting economic growth in neighborhoods like Wynwood.
moreContext: Miami's restaurant industry has been evolving from its traditional Cuban and Latin American dining identity toward a more globally diverse culinary scene. The Wynwood neighborhood specifically has transformed into a trendy dining and arts destination over the past decade, attracting upscale and experiential restaurants. Michelin's expanded coverage in Miami reflects the guide's recognition of the city's dining maturation. Specialty concepts like aperitivo bars and elevated coffee shops indicate consumers' growing interest in experiential, lifestyle-oriented dining beyond traditional meal categories.

title: A Traditional Italian Fave Made Even Better: Il Mulino
summary: Il Mulino New York located at Acqualina Resort, the Five Star beachfront property which recently celebrated its 20th anniversary, has always been one of my favorite traditional Italian restaurants in Miami. It’s authentic, gives good quality/value, the food is reliably very…
whatHappening: Il Mulino New York, the acclaimed Italian restaurant at Acqualina Resort in Miami, continues to deliver authentic traditional Italian cuisine. The establishment maintains its reputation as a reliable fine dining destination offering quality food and strong value.
whoInvolved: Il Mulino New York operates within Acqualina Resort, a five-star beachfront property in Miami that recently marked its 20th anniversary. The restaurant is part of the Il Mulino brand known for upscale Italian dining experiences.
whyMatters: Il Mulino represents the sustained appeal of classic Italian fine dining in Miami's competitive restaurant market. The restaurant's longevity and continued quality suggest strong demand for traditional Italian cuisine among both locals and resort guests.
moreContext: Acqualina Resort, celebrating two decades of operation, has established itself as a premier luxury beachfront destination in the Miami area. Il Mulino's presence at the resort enhances the property's dining portfolio and guest experience. The restaurant has earned recognition as one of Miami's preferred traditional Italian establishments, distinguished by its authenticity and consistency. Fine dining Italian restaurants remain a staple in Miami's upscale hospitality scene, competing for both destination diners and hotel guests seeking elevated culinary experiences.

title: Miami’s New Modern Mexican Hotspot—What You Need to Know
summary: We went to the Mexican Miami less than three weeks after opening and it was buzzy and many of its seats taken with individuals at the bar, small groups at high top round tables, and others headed to the private rooms celebrating a special occasion. The word has gotten out…
whatHappening: A new Modern Mexican restaurant has opened in Miami and is already generating significant buzz just three weeks after its launch. The venue is drawing crowds across its bar seating, high-top tables, and private dining areas.
whoInvolved: Mexican Miami is the restaurant behind this opening, though the article does not specify the ownership or leadership team. The establishment is operating in Miami's competitive dining scene.
whyMatters: The rapid popularity of the venue suggests strong market demand for Modern Mexican cuisine in Miami. Early success typically signals healthy restaurant economics and could influence future dining trends and investment in the area.
moreContext: Miami has a well-established dining culture with significant Mexican and Latin American culinary presence, making it a natural testing ground for elevated dining concepts. The restaurant is attracting diverse customer segments—solo diners at the bar, small social groups, and larger parties seeking private event spaces—indicating broad appeal. The fact that word-of-mouth has spread this quickly within three weeks suggests strong social media activity and positive early reviews are driving traffic. This opening reflects a broader trend of upscale, modern takes on traditional cuisines gaining traction in major metropolitan markets.
